human_verification.notes: Reviewed service agreement boundaries, home/field service agreement and warranty artifacts, DMEPOS repair and maintenance handling, authorization/referral/eligibility prerequisites, PHI controls, clinical and payer escalation rules, research-source authority mapping, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for agreement exceptions, backdated coverage, pricing, refunds, legal interpretation, warranty adjudication, recall remedies, technician execution, licensed trade signoff, safety release, clinical judgment, payer interpretation, authorization approval, coding, claims, and PHI disclosure.

Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, recognized healthcare workflow guidance, and public home-services consumer and warranty guidance.

Scenario map covers normal agreement progression, missing prerequisites, PHI handling, clinical/payer escalation, agreement exception refusal, DMEPOS warranty and repair boundary, incomplete artifacts, rework, completion evidence, low-confidence routing, policy conflict, and tenant terminology.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build, evidence, and approval event.
